title: salvia phlomoides subsp. africana
intro: Salvia phlomoides subsp. africana, also known as Cape sage or African sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia phlomoides plant, which belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Salvia phlomoides subsp. africana include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Salvia phlomoides subsp. africana can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
